anchor.snippet: is a mathematical function of a complex variable defined as

annotations.note: `riemannZeta : ℂ → ℂ` is defined directly in Mathlib as the analytically continued zeta function.

anchors.snippet: is a mathematical function of a complex variable defined as

annotations.label: Irrationality of ζ(3) (Apéry)

anchor.snippet: Roger Apéry proved the irrationality of

annotations.note: No irrationality result for ζ(3) (or any specific zeta value) exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Definition of ζ(s)

anchor.snippet: is a function of a complex variable

annotations.note: `riemannZeta` is the zeta function of a complex variable, with `zeta_eq_tsum_one_div_nat_cpow` giving the Dirichlet sum for Re s > 1.

annotations.label: Definition via analytic continuation

anchor.snippet: is defined for other complex values via analytic continuation

annotations.note: `riemannZeta` is constructed on all of ℂ via the (Hurwitz-zeta-based) analytic continuation.

annotations.label: Analytic continuation to s ≠ 1

anchor.snippet: can be continued analytically to all complex values

annotations.note: `differentiableAt_riemannZeta` proves ζ is holomorphic at every s ≠ 1.

anchors.snippet: can be continued analytically to all complex values

annotations.label: Meromorphic with simple pole at s=1

anchor.snippet: is a meromorphic function on the whole complex plane

annotations.note: `riemannZeta_residue_one` shows the residue at s=1 is 1 (simple pole), and `differentiableAt_riemannZeta`/`analyticOn_riemannZeta` give holomorphy elsewhere.

anchors.snippet: is a meromorphic function on the whole complex plane

annotations.label: Euler product formula

anchor.snippet: the connection between the zeta function and prime numbers was discovered by Euler, who proved the identity

annotations.note: `riemannZeta_eulerProduct_tprod` proves ∏ₚ (1 − p^(−s))⁻¹ = ζ(s) for Re s > 1.

annotations.label: Infinitely many primes

anchor.snippet: implies that there are infinitely many primes

annotations.note: Infinitude of primes is proven as `Nat.exists_infinite_primes`/`Nat.infinite_setOf_prime` (via Euclid, not the Euler product).

annotations.label: Sum of reciprocals of primes diverges

anchor.snippet: the sum of the reciprocals of the primes is infinite

annotations.note: `not_summable_one_div_on_primes` (and `Nat.Primes.not_summable_one_div`) prove the prime reciprocal sum diverges.

annotations.label: Density of primes is zero

anchor.snippet: the density of the set of primes within the set of positive integers is zero

annotations.note: The prime-counting function and an upper bound exist, but the asymptotic density limit π(n)/n → 0 is not proven.

annotations.label: Probability of coprimality

anchor.snippet: the asymptotic probability that s randomly selected integers within a bound are set-wise coprime

annotations.note: No theorem relating coprimality probability of s integers to 1/ζ(s) is present.

annotations.label: Riemann functional equation

anchor.snippet: This zeta function satisfies the functional equation

annotations.note: `riemannZeta_one_sub` is the asymmetric functional equation relating ζ(1−s) and ζ(s).

anchors.snippet: This zeta function satisfies the functional equation

annotations.label: Trivial zeros at negative even integers

anchor.snippet: has a simple zero at each even negative integer

annotations.note: `riemannZeta_neg_two_mul_nat_add_one` proves ζ(−2(n+1))=0, but the simplicity (order one) of these zeros is not formalized.

anchors.snippet: has a simple zero at each even negative integer

annotations.label: Symmetric functional equation (xi function)

anchor.snippet: Riemann also found a symmetric version of the functional equation by setting

annotations.note: `completedRiemannZeta_one_sub` proves Λ(1−s)=Λ(s) for the completed zeta Λ = π^(−s/2)Γ(s/2)ζ(s), the symmetric functional equation (Mathlib uses Λ rather than ξ).

anchors.snippet: Riemann also found a symmetric version of the functional equation by setting

annotations.label: Trivial zeros

anchor.section: Zeros, the critical line, and the Riemann hypothesis
anchor.snippet: These are called the trivial zeros

annotations.note: The trivial zeros are proven to be zeros via `riemannZeta_neg_two_mul_nat_add_one`, but there is no named definition isolating exactly the trivial-zero set.

annotations.label: Critical strip

anchor.section: Zeros, the critical line, and the Riemann hypothesis
anchor.snippet: which is called the critical strip

annotations.note: No definition of the critical strip 0 < Re s < 1 exists in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Critical line

anchor.section: Zeros, the critical line, and the Riemann hypothesis
anchor.snippet: is called the critical line

annotations.note: No definition of the critical line Re s = 1/2 is present (it appears only inline inside `RiemannHypothesis`).

annotations.label: Riemann hypothesis

anchor.section: Zeros, the critical line, and the Riemann hypothesis
anchor.snippet: asserts that all non-trivial zeros are on the critical line

annotations.note: `RiemannHypothesis` is defined as the Prop that every nontrivial zero has real part 1/2.

annotations.label: Conrey: >40% on critical line

anchor.section: Zeros, the critical line, and the Riemann hypothesis
anchor.snippet: Conrey proved that more than 40% of the non-trivial zeros

annotations.note: No proportion-of-zeros results are formalized.

annotations.note: No bound on the zero-counting function N(T) is present.

annotations.note: Hardy's theorem on infinitely many zeros on the critical line is not formalized.

annotations.label: PNT equivalent to no zeros on Re(s)=1

anchor.snippet: The prime number theorem is equivalent to the fact that there are no zeros of the zeta function on the line Re( s ) = 1

annotations.note: Non-vanishing on Re s = 1 is proven (`riemannZeta_ne_zero_of_one_le_re`), but the prime number theorem and the equivalence itself are not formalized.

annotations.label: Zeros symmetric about real axis

anchor.snippet: are symmetric about the real axis

annotations.note: No `riemannZeta (conj s) = conj (riemannZeta s)` (reflection across the real axis) lemma exists.

annotations.label: Nontrivial zeros symmetric about critical line

anchor.snippet: the non-trivial zeros are symmetric about the critical line

annotations.note: The functional equation `riemannZeta_one_sub`/`completedRiemannZeta_one_sub` underlies this symmetry, but symmetry of zeros about Re s = 1/2 is not stated as such.

annotations.label: No zeros on line Re(s)=1

anchor.snippet: no zeros lie on the line with real part 1

annotations.note: `riemannZeta_ne_zero_of_one_le_re` proves ζ(s) ≠ 0 for Re s ≥ 1, covering the line Re s = 1.

annotations.label: Reciprocal via Möbius function

anchor.snippet: The reciprocal of the zeta function may be expressed as a Dirichlet series over the Möbius function

annotations.note: `LSeries_zeta_mul_Lseries_moebius` proves ζ(s)·∑μ(n)n^(−s) = 1 for Re s > 1, equivalent to 1/ζ(s) = ∑μ(n)n^(−s).

annotations.note: Riemann's Hankel-contour integral representation valid for all s is not formalized.

annotations.label: Zeta via Jacobi theta function

anchor.snippet: The Riemann zeta function can be given by a Mellin transform

annotations.note: `completedRiemannZeta` is built from a Mellin transform of a theta kernel (`jacobiTheta₂`-based `evenKernel`), but the explicit ζ-via-theta Mellin formula is not stated at the ζ level.

annotations.label: Regularized theta expression

anchor.snippet: which is well defined for all s except 0 and 1

annotations.note: `differentiable_completedZeta₀` shows the regularized Λ₀ is entire and `completedRiemannZeta_eq` removes the s=0,1 poles, but the explicit regularized theta integral is not stated.

annotations.label: Laurent series about s=1

anchor.snippet: It can therefore be expanded as a Laurent series about s = 1

annotations.note: The principal-part (residue 1) and constant term (γ) are proven via `riemannZeta_residue_one`/`tendsto_riemannZeta_sub_one_div`, but the full Laurent expansion with Stieltjes constants is not.

annotations.label: Stieltjes constants

anchor.snippet: are called the Stieltjes constants

annotations.note: Only the 0th Stieltjes constant γ₀ = γ appears (`tendsto_riemannZeta_sub_one_div`); the general Stieltjes constants γₙ are not defined.
